questions.question: Which shortcut turns a to‑do block into a bullet point?

options.text: /turnbullet
options.explanation: Typing "/turnbullet" after the forward slash converts a to‑do into a bullet point.

options.text: /bullet
options.explanation: "/bullet" is not a recognized command for this transformation.

options.text: /list
options.explanation: "/list" creates a numbered list, not a bullet point from a to‑do.

options.text: /toggle
options.explanation: "/toggle" creates a toggle list, not a bullet point.

questions.question: How do you open the "Turn into" menu for a selected block?

options.text: Right‑click the block
options.explanation: Right‑click opens a context menu, but not the specific "Turn into" submenu.

options.text: Click the six‑dot icon that appears on hover
options.explanation: The six‑dot (⋮⋮) icon provides the "Turn into" option when clicked.

options.text: Double‑click the block
options.explanation: Double‑click puts the block into edit mode, not into the Turn into menu.

options.text: Press Enter while the block is selected
options.explanation: Enter confirms edits; it does not open the Turn into menu.

questions.question: What does the "Turn into page" feature allow you to do?

options.text: Convert a block into a separate page and choose where that page lives in the workspace
options.explanation: Turning a block into a page creates a new page and lets you place it anywhere you like.

options.text: Duplicate the block on the same page
options.explanation: Duplicating a block does not create a new page.

options.text: Export the block as a PDF document
options.explanation: Exporting is a separate action; Turn into page does not produce a PDF.

options.text: Lock the block so it can’t be edited
options.explanation: Locking is a different feature; Turn into page changes the block’s type.

questions.question: Which of the following actions will NOT change a block into another content type?

options.text: Highlight the block and use the formatting toolbar
options.explanation: The formatting toolbar includes options to transform the block.

options.text: Type a forward slash (/) followed by a transformation command
options.explanation: Slash commands are a primary way to change block types.

options.text: Click the six‑dot icon and select a new block type
options.explanation: The six‑dot menu provides the Turn into options.

options.text: Press Ctrl + S while the block is selected
options.explanation: Ctrl + S saves the page; it does not transform block types.

questions.question: To turn a paragraph into a callout block, which command should you type after the forward slash?

options.text: turncallout
options.explanation: The command must be entered with a leading slash.

options.text: /callout
options.explanation: "/callout" creates a callout block directly, not by transforming an existing paragraph.

options.text: /turncallout
options.explanation: The correct slash command is "/turncallout", which converts the paragraph into a callout.

options.text: callout
options.explanation: Without the slash, Notion treats it as plain text.

questions.question: Which benefit of transforming blocks is NOT mentioned in the guide?

options.text: Creates clearer, richer content faster
options.explanation: The guide explicitly mentions faster, clearer content creation.

options.text: Improves organization and navigation
options.explanation: Improved organization and navigation are highlighted benefits.

options.text: Enables automatic AI summarization of content
options.explanation: AI summarization is not discussed as a benefit of transforming blocks.

options.text: Helps you store information more efficiently
options.explanation: Storing information efficiently is implied through better organization.

questions.question: You have three separate to‑do items that need to become individual pages, each nested under a different parent page. Which sequence of actions is the most efficient way to accomplish this?

options.text: Highlight all three to‑dos, click the six‑dot icon, select "Turn into page", then drag each new page under its desired parent.
options.explanation: Turning multiple blocks into pages at once creates a single page with all content, not three separate pages.

options.text: Turn each to‑do into a page one‑by‑one, then use the Move option to place each new page under its respective parent.
options.explanation: Converting each to‑do individually ensures three distinct pages, and moving them lets you nest each under the correct parent.

options.text: Use the formatting toolbar to convert the to‑dos into bullet points, then select "Turn into page" on the bullet list.
options.explanation: Changing to bullet points first does not create separate pages; the Turn into page command works on the selected block(s) as they are.

options.text: Duplicate the three to‑dos, then use a template button to generate pages for each duplicate.
options.explanation: Template buttons are a more complex workaround and not the most efficient method for this simple task.
